rationale: Rindfleisch et al. (2008) is cited in the method section to justify the decision to use a two-wave longitudinal design, rather than only as general background. The citing paper returns to this citation when presenting its cross-sectional versus longitudinal comparison and explicitly references the cited paper's claims about common method variance and causal inference. The authors then use their own results to support that design logic, so the citation materially informs both study setup and interpretation.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Rindfleisch et al. (2008)'s methodological guidance on longitudinal versus cross-sectional survey design to its buyer-supplier context. It uses that framework to motivate a two-wave design and to interpret longitudinal modeling as providing stronger causal inference than a cross-sectional model.
